description_en: A process or set of rules to be followed in calculations or other problem-solving operations.
example: The search engine uses a complex algorithm to rank pages.

description_en: A structured set of data held in a computer, especially one that is accessible in various ways.
example: We need to update the customer database.
note: SQL and NoSQL are common types.

description_en: The process of converting information or data into a code, especially to prevent unauthorized access.
example: All confidential files are protected by strong encryption.

description_en: Tools, machinery, and other durable equipment.
example: The new software requires faster hardware to run smoothly.

description_en: A point where two systems, subjects, organizations, etc., meet and interact.
example: The user interface is very intuitive and easy to use.

description_en: A group or system of interconnected people or things.
example: The office network was down for two hours this morning.
note: Includes LAN, WAN, and the Internet.

description_en: The official procedure or system of rules governing affairs of state or diplomatic occasions.
example: HTTP is the standard protocol for web browsing.

description_en: A computer or computer program which manages access to a centralized resource or service in a network.
example: The website is hosted on a high-speed server.

description_en: The programs and other operating information used by a computer.
example: You need to install the latest software updates.

description_en: The action or method of storing something for future use.
example: The new smartphone has 128GB of internal storage.
note: Commonly measured in GB or TB.

description_en: The range of frequencies within a given band, in particular that used for transmitting a signal.
example: The video streaming requires a high-speed internet bandwidth.

description_en: A program with a graphical user interface for displaying HTML files.
example: Chrome is my favorite web browser.
note: Used to access the world wide web.

description_en: A hardware or software component that stores data so that future requests for that data can be served faster.
example: Clear the browser cache if you experience loading issues.

description_en: A network of remote servers hosted on the Internet and used to store, manage, and process data.
example: Many companies are moving their data to the cloud.

description_en: A program that converts instructions into a machine-code or lower-level form so that they can be read and executed by a computer.
example: The C++ compiler generated an error on line 50.
note: Essential for languages like C, Java.

description_en: The process of identifying and removing errors from computer hardware or software.
example: I spent all night debugging the new feature.
note: A core part of the development cycle.

description_en: The action of bringing resources into effective action.
example: The deployment to the production server was successful.
note: Last stage of the release cycle.

description_en: A network security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic.
example: Make sure your firewall is enabled to protect your PC.

description_en: A basic structure underlying a system, concept, or text.
example: Laravel is a popular PHP framework.

description_en: Enabling a person to discover or learn something for themselves.
example: Antivirus software often use heuristic scanning for new threats.
note: Common in AI and search algorithms.

description_en: The repetition of a process or utterance.
example: The design improved significantly in the second iteration.

description_en: The delay before a transfer of data begins following an instruction for its transfer.
example: Low latency is critical for online gaming.

description_en: Software that acts as a bridge between an operating system or database and applications.
example: The middleware handles user authentication for our API.

description_en: The action of making the best or most effective use of a situation or resource.
example: Code optimization can reduce the server response time.

description_en: A numerical or other measurable factor forming one of a set that defines a system or sets the conditions of its operation.
example: You need to pass the correct parameters to the function.

description_en: A question, especially one addressed to an official or organization.
example: The SQL query took five seconds to execute.
note: Used to fetch data from databases.

description_en: A central location in which data is stored and managed.
example: I pushed my latest changes to the GitHub repository.

description_en: The capacity to be changed in size or scale.
example: Our application was designed with horizontal scalability in mind.

description_en: The arrangement of words and phrases to create well-formed sentences in a language.
example: Python has a clean and simple syntax.

description_en: A thing serving as a visible or tangible representation of a fact, quality, feeling, etc.
example: The API requires an authentication token in the header.

description_en: The state of being useful, profitable, or beneficial.
example: Disk Cleanup is a useful system utility.

description_en: The action of checking or proving the validity or accuracy of something.
example: Form validation prevents empty submissions.

description_en: The act of creating a virtual version of something, such as an operating system.
example: Server virtualization helps reduce hardware costs.

description_en: The sequence of industrial, administrative, or other processes through which a piece of work passes from initiation to completion.
example: Using Git simplified our team's internal workflow.

description_en: The part of a computer system or application that is not directly accessed by the user.
example: The backend was built using Node.js and MongoDB.

description_en: The part of a computer system or application that is directly accessed by the user.
example: A frontend developer works with HTML, CSS, and JS.
note: The user-facing side of an app.

description_en: Permanent software programmed into a read-only memory.
example: The router's firmware needs a security patch update.

description_en: The maximum rate of data transfer across a given path.
example: Fiber optic offers much higher bandwidth than DSL.

description_en: A small piece of data sent from a website and stored on the user's computer.
example: Cookies are used to remember your login session.

description_en: Process of encoding messages so only authorized parties can read it.
example: End-to-end encryption ensures only you and the recipient can see messages.

description_en: How data is stored and retrieved from a storage medium.
example: NTFS is the default filesystem for Windows.

description_en: A node in a computer network that serves as an entrance to another network.
example: The default gateway is often the IP of your router.

description_en: A computer that mediates multiple users' access to a computer network.
example: Which company do you use to host your website?

description_en: A database feature that speeds up data retrieval.
example: Adding an index to the email column made searches much faster.

description_en: The core of a computer's operating system, having complete control over everything in the system.
example: The Linux kernel is open source and very flexible.

description_en: Software that is specifically designed to disrupt, damage, or gain unauthorized access.
example: Don't click on suspicious links to avoid malware.

description_en: Any device connected to a computer network.
example: The blockchain network consists of thousands of independent nodes.

description_en: The software that supports a computer's basic functions.
example: Windows and macOS are the most popular operating systems for PCs.

description_en: A formatted unit of data carried by a packet-switched network.
example: Network data is broken into packets before being sent.

description_en: The period during which a computer program is executing.
example: The error only occurs at runtime, not during compile-time.
